quests.title: Counting Mangoes at Lumley Beach
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P1-MA: Counting and simple addition to 20

questions.questionText: Amie has 3 mangoes in her basket. Fatima gives her 2 more mangoes. How many mangoes does Amie have now?
questions.explanation: 3 + 2 = 5 mangoes. When we add, we put numbers together to find the total.
questions.krioInstruction: Amie gɛt 3 mango. Fatima gi am 2 mɔ. Wetin i gɛt naw? 3 + 2 = 5 mango!

questions.questionText: There are 8 children playing on Lumley Beach. 3 children go home. How many children are still playing?
questions.explanation: 8 - 3 = 5 children. When we subtract, we take away from the group to find what is left.
questions.krioInstruction: 8 pikin de play. 3 pikin go ose. Aw mɛni pikin lɛf? 8 - 3 = 5 pikin!

quests.title: Plants Around Our Home
quests.subject: General Science
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P1-SC: Basic plant parts and their functions

questions.questionText: Which part of a plant takes in water from the soil underground?
questions.explanation: Roots grow underground and absorb water and nutrients from the soil to feed the plant.
questions.krioInstruction: Wetin pɔl di wata fɔ di graɔn foh di plant? Na di ruts dem! Dem de ondaneet di graɔn.

questions.questionText: What do green leaves need from the sun to make food for the plant?
questions.explanation: Leaves use sunlight, water, and air to make food through a process called photosynthesis.
questions.krioInstruction: Di lif dem nyam sunlait foh mek fud foh di plant. Dat na kɔl fotosintesis!

quests.title: My Family and Community
quests.subject: Social Studies & Civics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P1-SS: Family roles and community belonging

questions.questionText: Who is the person in your family who gives birth to you and takes care of you?
questions.explanation: A mother is the woman who gives birth to her children and cares for the family with love.
questions.krioInstruction: Na yu mama de bɔn yu and tek ker foh yu. I de lov yu plɛnti!

questions.questionText: What do we call a group of people living and helping each other in the same area?
questions.explanation: A community is a group of people living and working together in the same area, helping one another.
questions.krioInstruction: We plɛnti pipul de liv togɛda an elp ɔda, wi kɔl dat wan 'community'. Na wi town!

quests.title: Vowels: A, E, I, O, U
quests.subject: English Language
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P1-EL: Recognising vowels and consonants

questions.questionText: Which of these letters is a VOWEL?
questions.explanation: The five vowels in the English alphabet are A, E, I, O, and U. All other letters are consonants.
questions.krioInstruction: Di vɔl dɛm na A, E, I, O, U. Ɔl di ɔda lɛta dɛm na kɔnsɔnant!

questions.questionText: The word 'OPEN' starts with which vowel?
questions.explanation: The word OPEN starts with the letter O, which is one of the five vowels: A, E, I, O, U.
questions.krioInstruction: Di wɔd OPEN stat wit 'O'. O na wan vɔl. Kɛn yu sɛ ɔl faiv vɔl dɛm?

quests.title: Sharing Sweet Potatoes Equally
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P1-MA: Equal sharing as introduction to division

questions.questionText: Ibrahim has 6 sweet potatoes. He shares them equally between himself and his sister. How many does each person get?
questions.explanation: 6 sweet potatoes shared equally between 2 people: 6 ÷ 2 = 3 potatoes each.
questions.krioInstruction: Ibrahim gɛt 6 sɔt pateto. I mek tu pat foh im an in sista. Aw mɛni ɛch wan gɛt? 6 ÷ 2 = 3!

questions.questionText: Mama has 10 groundnuts. She puts them into 2 equal groups. How many groundnuts are in each group?
questions.explanation: 10 groundnuts divided into 2 equal groups = 5 groundnuts in each group. 10 ÷ 2 = 5.
questions.krioInstruction: 10 graɔnnat divaid bai 2 = 5 foh ɛch gruf. Mama glad sey i mek am ɛkwɔl!

quests.title: Adding Peppers at Kenema Market
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P2-MA: Two-digit addition without regrouping

questions.questionText: A market seller has 23 peppers in one tray and 14 peppers in another tray. How many peppers does she have in total?
questions.explanation: 23 + 14 = 37 peppers. Add the units first: 3+4=7, then the tens: 2+1=3, giving 37.
questions.krioInstruction: 23 papɛ plos 14 papɛ = 37 papɛ. Fɔst yus di wan dɛm, dɛn di tɛn dɛm. 3+4=7, 2+1=3 = 37!

questions.questionText: A Class 2 school has 15 boys and 22 girls. How many pupils are there altogether?
questions.explanation: 15 + 22 = 37 pupils. Units: 5+2=7, Tens: 1+2=3, Total = 37.
questions.krioInstruction: 15 bɔyz plos 22 gɔlz = 37 pikin. 5+2=7 fɔ di wan, 1+2=3 fɔ di tɛn. Wi gɛt 37!

quests.title: Wild Animals of Sierra Leone
quests.subject: General Science
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P2-SC: Classifying animals by features

questions.questionText: The chimpanzee lives in Sierra Leone's rainforests. Is the chimpanzee a mammal or a fish?
questions.explanation: Chimpanzees are mammals — they have hair, give birth to live young, and feed their babies milk.
questions.krioInstruction: Chimpanzee na mɛmɔl. I gɛt yɛ, bɔn im pikin alayv, an i gi dem mɔlk. Nɔ fish am!

questions.questionText: Which animal lives in water and uses gills to breathe?
questions.explanation: Fish live in water and breathe using gills, which absorb oxygen directly from the water.
questions.krioInstruction: Fish de liv na wata an i yus gil foh brɛt. Di gil tek oksijin fɔh di wata. Dat na aw fish sɔvaiv!

quests.title: People Who Help Our Town
quests.subject: Social Studies & Civics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P2-SS: Community helpers and their roles

questions.questionText: Who do you visit when you are sick and need medicine?
questions.explanation: Doctors and nurses work in hospitals and clinics to help sick people get better.
questions.krioInstruction: Wen yu sik, go si di dɔkta ɔ nos. Dem de wok na aspital foh mek yu bɛta. Dem elp wi!

questions.questionText: Who teaches children to read, write, and count at school?
questions.explanation: Teachers are important community helpers who educate children in schools every day.
questions.krioInstruction: Di ticha na wan impɔtant pɔsin na wi kɔmyuniti. I lɛn wi rid, rait, an kaɔnt. Tɛnki ticha!

quests.title: Building Simple Sentences
quests.subject: English Language
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P2-EL: Sentence structure and punctuation

questions.questionText: Which of these is a complete sentence?
questions.explanation: A sentence needs a subject (the dog) and a verb (runs). It starts with a capital letter and ends with a full stop.
questions.krioInstruction: Wan gɔd sɛntɛns nid wan sabdʒekt (di dɔg) an wan vɛb (runs). I stat bɪg lɛta an ɛnd wit fɔl stɔp.

questions.questionText: What punctuation mark goes at the END of a question?
questions.explanation: Questions always end with a question mark (?). For example: What is your name?
questions.krioInstruction: Ɔltem wen yu aks kwɛschɔn, put kwɛschɔn mak (?) na di ɛnd. Lɛk: Wetin na yu nem?

quests.title: Telling the Time at School
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P2-MA: Reading analogue clocks to the hour and half hour

questions.questionText: School starts at 8 o'clock. On a clock showing exactly 8 o'clock, where does the SHORT hand (hour hand) point?
questions.explanation: The short hand (hour hand) points to the hour number. At 8 o'clock, it points to the 8. The long minute hand points to 12.
questions.krioInstruction: Di shɔt an (yɛ an) de pɔint to di yɛ nɔmba. Foh 8 ɔkɔlɔk, i pɔint to 8. Di lɔng minit an de pɔint to 12!

questions.questionText: If it is half past 3 (3:30), where does the LONG hand (minute hand) point?
questions.explanation: At half past any hour, the minute hand always points to the 6, because 30 minutes is half of 60 minutes.
questions.krioInstruction: Na haf pɛs, di minit an de pɔint to di 6. 30 minit na haf ɔwa. 3:30 na 'haf pɛs tri'!

quests.title: Kru Town Market Commerce
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P3-MA: Multiplication and money problems in local context

questions.questionText: Mariama sells cassava bread in Kru Town Market. She starts with 15 fresh loaves and sells 9. How many loaves are left in her tray?
questions.explanation: 15 loaves minus 9 sold leaves Mariama with 6 loaves to sell later.
questions.krioInstruction: Mariama bin gɛt 15 bray; i səl 9. Wetin lɛf na di tray? 15 minus 9 na 6. Prɛs '6' foh win!

questions.questionText: If each loaf of cassava bread sells for 20 Leones, how many Leones does Mariama earn by selling 3 loaves?
questions.explanation: Selling 3 loaves at 20 Leones each equals 3 x 20 = 60 Leones.
questions.krioInstruction: Ɛvry loaf na 20 Leone. If i səl 3 foh di fambul dɛm, wetin i gɛt? 3 x 20 Leone na 60 Leone.

quests.title: The Water Cycle by Rokel River
quests.subject: General Science
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P3-SC: The water cycle — evaporation, condensation, precipitation

questions.questionText: When the sun heats water in the Rokel River, the water turns into vapour and rises into the air. What is this process called?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is when liquid water is heated by the sun and turns into water vapour (gas) that rises into the atmosphere.
questions.krioInstruction: Wen di sɔn it di wata an di wata tɔn to vapɔ an go op, dat na evapɔreshɔn. Di wata de kom pɔ fɔ di Rokel!

questions.questionText: Water vapour in the atmosphere cools and turns back into tiny water droplets, forming clouds. What is this called?
questions.explanation: Condensation is when water vapour cools and turns back into liquid water droplets, forming clouds in the sky.
questions.krioInstruction: Wen di vapɔ kul dong an tɔn bɛk to wata drɔplɛts an fɔm klɔd, dat na kondɛnseshɔn. Dat na aw wi gɛt klɔd!

quests.title: Regions of Sierra Leone
quests.subject: Social Studies & Civics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P3-SS: Administrative divisions of Sierra Leone

questions.questionText: Sierra Leone is divided into how many main provinces plus the Western Area?
questions.explanation: Sierra Leone has three provinces (Northern, Southern, Eastern) plus the Western Area where the capital Freetown is located.
questions.krioInstruction: Salone gɛt 3 provins (Nɔtɛn, Sɔtɛn, Istɛn) plos di Wɛstɛn Eria we Fritɔn de. Dat fɔ pɔlis in ɔl!

questions.questionText: In which province is the city of Kenema located?
questions.explanation: Kenema is one of the major cities in Sierra Leone's Eastern Province, known for its diamond mining industry.
questions.krioInstruction: Kenema de na di Istɛn Provins. I na wan impɔtant siti fɔ dayamɔn mayning na Salone!

quests.title: Nouns and Verbs in Sentences
quests.subject: English Language
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P3-EL: Identifying nouns and verbs in sentences

questions.questionText: In the sentence 'The girl READS a book', which word is the VERB (action word)?
questions.explanation: A verb is an action word. 'Reads' is what the girl is doing — it describes the action, making it the verb.
questions.krioInstruction: Di vɛb na di akshɔn wɔd. Na 'reads' di gɔl de du. So 'reads' na di vɛb na di sɛntɛns!

questions.questionText: Which of these words is a NOUN (naming word for a place)?
questions.explanation: A noun names a person, place, or thing. Freetown is the capital city of Sierra Leone — it is a place, so it is a noun.
questions.krioInstruction: Fritɔn na wan pɛs (naɔn). Di ɔda wɔd dɛm (jump, run) na vɛb dɛm. Fritɔn na naɔn bikos i na wan siti!

quests.title: Shapes at Makeni Junction
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P3-MA: Properties of 2D shapes

questions.questionText: A traffic sign near Makeni Junction is shaped like a triangle. How many sides does a triangle have?
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides and 3 angles. The word 'tri' means three, which is why we call it a triangle.
questions.krioInstruction: Di traiangl gɛt 3 said an 3 kɔna. 'Tri' min tɛri. Na dat mek wi kɔl am tri-angl!

questions.questionText: Which shape has 4 equal sides and 4 right-angle corners?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides and 4 right angles (90° corners). It differs from a rectangle which has 2 long and 2 short sides.
questions.krioInstruction: Di skwɛ gɛt fɔ ɛkwɔl said an fɔ rait-angl kɔna. Rɛktangl gɛt 2 lɔng said an 2 shɔt said. Skwɛ ɔltem ɛkwɔl!

quests.title: Fractions at Bonthe Fishing Village
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P4-MA: Understanding fractions — halves, quarters, thirds

questions.questionText: A fisherman at Bonthe catches 12 fish and divides them into 4 equal groups. What fraction represents one group?
questions.explanation: When something is divided into 4 equal parts, each part is one quarter (1/4). 12 ÷ 4 = 3 fish per group.
questions.krioInstruction: 12 fis divaid ɛkwɔl bai 4 = 3 fis foh ɛch pɔt. Wan pɔt aɔt ɔv 4 na wan kwɔta (1/4)!

questions.questionText: A rice bag is divided in half. If one half weighs 25 kg, how heavy is the full rice bag?
questions.explanation: If one half (1/2) weighs 25 kg, then the whole bag = 2 × 25 = 50 kg. Double the half to get the whole.
questions.krioInstruction: Wan haf (1/2) na 25 kilogram. Di wol bɛg = 2 × 25 = 50 kilogram. Tubul di haf tu gɛt di hol!

quests.title: Gola Rain Forest Giants
quests.subject: General Science
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P4-SC: Wildlife conservation and endangered species

questions.questionText: The rare Western Pygmy Hippo lives in Sierra Leone's Gola Rainforest. Is the Pygmy Hippo considered an endangered species or a common household pet?
questions.explanation: Pygmy Hippos are endangered, meaning very few are left in the wild and we must preserve their rainforest home.
questions.krioInstruction: Di tinap-tinap Pygmy Hippo rɛb na bush dɛm! Wi foh mɛnj am foh ya bikos i de foh dɔn. I na endangered spɛshis.

questions.questionText: What key resource do chimpanzees and rare monkeys find on Tiwai Island in the Moa River?
questions.explanation: Tiwai Island is a world-renowned wildlife sanctuary offering a protected natural rainforest habitat.
questions.krioInstruction: Tiwai Island na di Moa Riva na rich bush we de protɛkt di bɔbɔ chimpanzee dɛm fɔh hɔntin. Na sanctuary.

quests.title: Sierra Leone's Independence 1961
quests.subject: Social Studies & Civics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P4-SS: Sierra Leone's path to independence

questions.questionText: On which date did Sierra Leone gain independence from British colonial rule?
questions.explanation: Sierra Leone became an independent nation on April 27, 1961. This date is celebrated each year as Independence Day.
questions.krioInstruction: Salone gɛt independence pan April 27, 1961. Ɛvry yɛ wi sɛlɛbreit dis speshɔl dei! Fri Salone!

questions.questionText: Who was Sierra Leone's first Prime Minister when the country gained independence in 1961?
questions.explanation: Sir Milton Margai became Sierra Leone's first Prime Minister when the country gained independence from Britain on April 27, 1961.
questions.krioInstruction: Sɛ Milton Margai bin na Salone fɔs Praym Minista wen wi gɛt independence. I fɔt foh fridom!

quests.title: Adjectives Describe Our World
quests.subject: English Language
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P4-EL: Adjectives and descriptive language

questions.questionText: In the sentence 'The TALL mango tree grows near the river', which word is the ADJECTIVE?
questions.explanation: An adjective is a describing word. 'Tall' describes the mango tree, telling us what kind of tree it is.
questions.krioInstruction: Adjektiv na di wɔd we dɛskraib wan naɔn. 'Tall' dɛskraib di mango tri. I tel wi wetin kayn tri am na!

questions.questionText: Which of these words is an ADJECTIVE that describes colour?
questions.explanation: 'Green' is an adjective that describes colour. It is a describing word that tells us what colour something is, like 'the green banana'.
questions.krioInstruction: Di wɔd 'green' na adjektiv kɔlɔ. I tel wi wetin kɔlɔ wan ting na. Lɛk 'di grɛn grɛs' ɔ 'di grɛn bananɛ'!

quests.title: Rainy Season and Dry Season in Salone
quests.subject: General Science
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P4-SC: Tropical climate and seasonal patterns

questions.questionText: Which months are typically part of the Rainy Season in Sierra Leone?
questions.explanation: Sierra Leone's rainy season usually runs from May to November, with the heaviest rains in July and August.
questions.krioInstruction: Salone Rɛni Sizɔn de kɔm fɔm Mɛi go to Novɛmba. Di ren ɛvi foh Julai an Ɔgɔst. Wi gɛt plɛnti wata!

questions.questionText: What type of climate does Sierra Leone have, with warm temperatures and two distinct seasons?
questions.explanation: Sierra Leone has a tropical climate with warm temperatures throughout the year and two seasons — wet (rainy) and dry.
questions.krioInstruction: Salone gɛt trɔpikɔl klaymat. I wɔm ɔltaim wit tu sizɔn: di wɛt (rɛni) sizɔn an di dray sizɔn!

quests.title: Historic Cotton Tree & Bai Bureh
quests.subject: Social Studies & Civics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P5-SS: Sierra Leonean resistance heroes and symbols of freedom

questions.questionText: Which brave Sierra Leonean ruler and strategist led the 1898 Hut Tax Rebellion against British administration?
questions.explanation: Bai Bureh was from Kasseh and successfully defended his territory during the British Hut Tax War.
questions.krioInstruction: Di brayv lida we fɛt di britis dɛm foh di hut tɔks na bin Bai Bureh!

questions.questionText: Under which majestic national symbol in Freetown did early settlers and freed slaves gather to pray for freedom?
questions.explanation: The historic Freetown Cotton Tree stood for centuries in Central Freetown, representing liberty, resilience, and community.
questions.krioInstruction: Wi fambul dɛm we friman sɔt dɔn de wɔship and pre fɔh fridom na di rɔyal Kɔtin Tri dɔn Fritɔn.

quests.title: Percentages at Port Loko Market
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P5-MA: Calculating percentages in everyday contexts

questions.questionText: A trader sells rice bags for Le 1000 each and offers a 10% discount. How much does a customer SAVE?
questions.explanation: 10% of Le 1000 = 10 ÷ 100 × 1000 = Le 100. The customer saves Le 100 and pays Le 900.
questions.krioInstruction: 10% ɔv Le 1000 = 10 ÷ 100 × 1000 = Le 100. Di kastaɔ sev Le 100 an pɛ Le 900. Gɔd dil!

questions.questionText: In a class of 40 pupils, 25 passed the NPSE exam. What PERCENTAGE of pupils passed?
questions.explanation: Percentage passed = (25 ÷ 40) × 100 = 62.5%. Always divide the part by the whole, then multiply by 100.
questions.krioInstruction: Pɛsɛntij = (25 ÷ 40) × 100 = 62.5%. 62.5% ɔv di klas pɔs di ekzam. Kɔngrachuleshɔn!

quests.title: The Human Digestive System
quests.subject: General Science
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P5-SC: Organs and functions of the human digestive system

questions.questionText: After we chew food in the mouth and swallow, it travels down the oesophagus to reach which organ where acid breaks it down?
questions.explanation: When we swallow, food travels down the oesophagus (food pipe) to the stomach where acid helps break it down further.
questions.krioInstruction: Wen wi swalow di fud, i go dong di ɔsɔfagɔs go reach di stɔmak. Di stɔmak yus asid foh brek dong di fud mɔ!

questions.questionText: Which part of the digestive system absorbs nutrients from digested food into the bloodstream?
questions.explanation: The small intestine absorbs nutrients from food after the stomach has broken it down. These nutrients enter the bloodstream to feed the body.
questions.krioInstruction: Di smɔl intɛstin nyam di nyutrɪnt dɛm fɔh di fud afta di stɔmak brek am dong. Di nyutrɪnt dɛm go na di blɔd foh fid di bɔdi!

quests.title: Reading About Sierra Leonean Heroes
quests.subject: English Language
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P5-EL: Reading comprehension and main idea identification

questions.questionText: Madam Yoko was a powerful Mende queen. What was she primarily known for achieving?
options: Building the first school in Freetown
questions.explanation: Madam Yoko was a skilled political leader who united Mende chiefdoms in the late 1800s through alliances and became Paramount Chief of Senehun.
questions.krioInstruction: Madam Yoko bin wan pawaful kwin we yunayt Mɛnde chifdɔm dem tru dipɔmasi. I bin Paramɔnt Chif ɔv Senehun!

questions.questionText: In reading comprehension, what is the MAIN IDEA of a passage?
options: The most important point the writer is making
Every single detail in the story
questions.correctOption: The most important point the writer is making
questions.explanation: The main idea is the central message or the most important point that the author wants to communicate in a passage.
questions.krioInstruction: Di mɛn aydiya na di mɔs impɔtant pɔint di raya de trɛ tel yu. Ɔltem luk foh am na di fɔs ɔ las pɛragraf!

quests.title: Area and Perimeter of Our School
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P5-MA: Calculating area and perimeter of rectangles

questions.questionText: A rectangular school garden is 8 metres long and 5 metres wide. What is the PERIMETER of the garden?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width) = 2 × (8 + 5) = 2 × 13 = 26 metres. Perimeter is the total distance around the edge.
questions.krioInstruction: Perimeter = 2 × (lɛngt + wit) = 2 × (8 + 5) = 2 × 13 = 26 mita. Di Pɛrimita na di tɔtɔl ɔrɔn di ɛj!

questions.questionText: A classroom floor is 10 metres long and 6 metres wide. What is the AREA of the classroom floor?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 10 × 6 = 60 square metres. Area is measured in square units and tells us the space inside a shape.
questions.krioInstruction: Eria = lɛngt × wit = 10 × 6 = 60 skwɛ mita. Eria tel wi aw mɛni skwɛ unit fit insayd wan shɛp!

quests.title: Ratios at Port Loko Salt Flats
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P6-MA: Ratios and proportional reasoning

questions.questionText: At Port Loko salt flats, for every 3 bags produced, 2 are exported and 1 is kept for local sale. What is the ratio of exported to local sale bags?
questions.explanation: The ratio of exported to local = 2 to 1, written as 2:1. For every 2 bags exported, 1 bag stays for local use.
questions.krioInstruction: 2 bɛg foh ekspɔt tu 1 bɛg foh lɔkɔl = ratio 2:1. Foh ɛvri 2 bɛg we go abrɔd, 1 stɛ foh ous!

questions.questionText: A recipe needs sugar and flour in the ratio 1:3. If you use 200g of sugar, how much flour do you need?
questions.explanation: Ratio 1:3 means for every 1 part sugar, use 3 parts flour. So 200g sugar × 3 = 600g flour.
questions.krioInstruction: Ratio 1:3 min foh ɛvri 1 pɔt shuga, yus 3 pɔt flaɔ. 200g shuga × 3 = 600g flaɔ. Rat!

quests.title: Solids, Liquids and Gases
quests.subject: General Science
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P6-SC: States of matter and changes of state

questions.questionText: When water is frozen at 0°C it becomes ice. What STATE OF MATTER is ice?
questions.explanation: Ice is a solid — it has a fixed shape and fixed volume. Water becomes a solid when its temperature drops to 0°C (freezing point).
questions.krioInstruction: Aas na sɔlid. I gɛt fiks shep an fiks volum. Wata tɔn sɔlid wen i kul dong to 0°C (frizing pɔint)!

questions.questionText: When water is heated to 100°C, it turns into steam. What state of matter is steam?
questions.explanation: Steam is water in its gas state (water vapour). Water becomes a gas when heated to its boiling point of 100°C.
questions.krioInstruction: Stim na wata na di gas steit. Wen wata it op to 100°C (boiling pɔint), i tɔn to stim. Gas nɔ gɛt fiks shep!

quests.title: Sierra Leone's Three Branches of Government
quests.subject: Social Studies & Civics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P6-SS: Structure and functions of Sierra Leone's government

questions.questionText: Sierra Leone's government has three branches. Which branch makes the laws of the country?
questions.explanation: The Legislature (Parliament) is the branch that makes and passes laws. Sierra Leone's Parliament is located in Freetown.
questions.krioInstruction: Di Lejilechɔ (Pɔliament) na di branch we mek di lɔ dɛm foh di kantri. Salone Pɔliament de na Fritɔn!

questions.questionText: Which branch of government interprets and applies the laws of Sierra Leone?
questions.explanation: The Judiciary (courts and judges) interprets and applies the laws. The Supreme Court is Sierra Leone's highest court.
questions.krioInstruction: Di Judishɛri (kɔt dɛm) na di branch we ɛsplɛn an aplai di lɔ dɛm. Di Suprɛm Kɔt na di ayɛs kɔt na Salone!

quests.title: Analysing a Poem About Sierra Leone
quests.subject: English Language
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P6-EL: Poetry analysis — stanzas, rhyme, theme

questions.questionText: When analysing a poem, what do we call the groups of lines separated by spaces (similar to paragraphs in prose)?
questions.explanation: A stanza is a group of lines in a poem, separated from other groups by blank spaces — similar to a paragraph in prose writing.
questions.krioInstruction: Wan stanza na wan gruf ɔv layns na wan pɔm. I sep fɔm di ɔda gruf bai wan blɛnk speis. Lɛk pɛragraf fɔ proz!

questions.questionText: In the poem lines 'Sierra Leone, sweet homeland / Your mountains stand so grand', which two words RHYME?
questions.explanation: 'Homeland' and 'grand' rhyme because both end with the same sound (-and). Rhyme is when the ending sounds of words match.
questions.krioInstruction: 'Homeland' an 'grand' raym bikos dem ɛnd wit di sem saond (-and, -and). Raym na wen di ɛndin saond ɔv wɔds mɛch!

quests.title: Reading School Data and Graphs
quests.subject: Mathematics
quests.alignedMbsseOutcome: MBSSE P6-MA: Interpreting bar graphs and using percentages with data

questions.questionText: A bar graph shows: Class 4 has 45 pupils, Class 5 has 38 pupils, Class 6 has 42 pupils. How many pupils are there in total?
questions.explanation: 45 + 38 + 42 = 125 pupils in total. Reading and adding data from a bar graph is a key mathematical skill.
questions.krioInstruction: 45 + 38 + 42 = 125 pikin in tɔtɔl. We wi rid data fɔm wan bɛ graf, wi ad ɔp ɔl di nɔmba dɛm foh gɛt di tɔtɔl!

questions.questionText: A survey shows 60% of 200 pupils prefer Mathematics. How many pupils prefer Mathematics?
questions.explanation: 60% of 200 = 60 ÷ 100 × 200 = 120 pupils prefer Mathematics. Using percentages with real data helps us understand information.
questions.krioInstruction: 60% ɔv 200 = 60 ÷ 100 × 200 = 120 pikin layk Mɛtimɛtiks. Pɛsɛntij elp wi ɔndastand real data!
